venues you can find great food explore the Great Ocean Road hang out in the Botanical Gardens and so much more so hit that Thumbs Up And subscribe button and let's Dive Right In This is my top 10 places to visit and things to do in Melbourne Australia first up on the list is Melbourne's Royal Botanic Gardens these are the most beautiful botanical gardens I've ever seen and they're located right in the middle of the city so you get this beautiful and unique contrast of City skyscrapers sneaking above the incredible plant life and lakes in the park these Gardens are lush green Oasis stretching across 94 Acres it's the perfect spot to unwind surrounded by Nature it's like stepping into a peaceful Paradise just a few feet from the hustle and bustle I went on a run here one of our mornings in Melvin and it was just amazing a great escape from the city but also just a few minutes walk from our Airbnb the park is free to enter but it is worth noting that there's events that happen here like movie nights and those are all ticketed events next we're getting high above the city and visiting the melin Sky Deck this observation deck is on the 88th floor of the Eureka Tower this spot offers breathtaking 360° views that stretch as far as your eye can see to visit it's 28 AUD for adults 17 AUD for children for to 16 and there is a family price for two adults and two children for 73 AUD the Sky Deck is open from 12:00 to 9:00 p.m. but I think that this is the perfect place to go to during sunset or just after Sunset I always enjoy seeing the hustle and bustle of a city at night time and the colors of the city just pop across the busy streets so seeing it from above is really cool and if you're an adventure lover there's additional extras that you can purchase while visiting the Sky Deck there is a VR experience and there is also something called The Edge which is a must try it's a glass cube that extends out from the building it's almost 300 m above the ground we didn't do this when we visited but we have done this at other observation decks and it's a very cool experience looking at the city below just through a thin piece of glass that you're standing on at number three just a short trip from Melvin City Center we're heading to the colorful bright and bathing boxes these 82 vibrant wooden Huts line in the beach and are a photographer's dream each box is uniquely painted adding a splash of color to the stunning Coastal backdrop they've been around since the 19th century originally serving as changing rooms for beachgoers but today they're a symbol of Melbourne's Charming Seaside culture the boxes themselves are privately owned so you cannot go inside however the beach is free to visit and it's a great place to unwind for an afternoon or Sunset hang and in the distance the colorful boxes are backdropped by Melvin Skyline and unique destination seeing the skyscrapers in the background of this Beach next up on the list is the perfect spot for art enthusiasts we're heading to the National Gallery of Victoria Australia's oldest and most visited Art Museum its collection is incredibly diverse featuring everything from classical masterpiece pieces to cuttingedge contemporary Works through its Halls you'll discover art from every corner of the globe showcasing centuries of creativity plus the gallery itself is an architectural Marvel making your visit an inspiring journey through both art and history the National Gallery is free to visit and it's open from 10:00 a.m. to 5:00 p.m. sometimes there are exhibitions and these sometimes have fees that vary based on the exhibition but they're typically between 20 to 30 AUD and really quick I want to mention that Bri and I have an entire series of Vlogs on the channel from this Australia trip we drove 50 hours across the east coast of the country hiking the tallest mountain in the continent visiting the world's oldest rainforest scuba diving in the Great Barrier Reef a Scenic flight over the wit Sundays and so much more plus we have travel guides top 10s and videos on the channel from all around the world so make sure to subscribe and stick around at number five we're going to be doing a day trip to Philip Island National Park to get here it's a 90-minute one-way drive from downtown Melbourne the drive is beautiful once you get out of and my favorite thing here that Bri and I did was we got our eyes photographed which may sound weird but there's a booth where a professional photographer takes a close up macro photo of your pupil it's pupils if you look at it right away we thought that this was really cool and it's probably one of our most unique pieces of wall art that we have in our house next up is Federation Square which is melin's cultural heart a place right in the middle of downtown where art culture and events come together the architecture in the square is beautiful and it's backdr by the surrounding sky the world of film and digital culture which was my favorite gallery or Museum experience in the entire city it celebrates film television video games and digital culture through engaging exhibitions screenings and interactive experiences making it a must visit for anyone interested in the world of moving images it's free for General exhibitions but there are fees for special exhibitions and events and then you can also in Federation Square explore the Ian powder Center for an incredible collection of Australian art which is also free to visit but has fees for certain exhibitions our final stop is the Majestic St Paul's Cathedral this Gothic Cathedral stands proudly in the heart of Melbourne just next to Federation Square the cathedral spes offer a striking contrast against the modern Skyline and inside you'll find stunning stained glass windows intricate Woodwork and a peaceful Ambiance so whether you're here to admire the architectural beauty or to experience a moment of of these churches and Cathedrals last up is a bonus destination and it's going to be a road trip to the 12 apostles these are a collection of dramatic limestone rocks off of the shore of Port Campbell videos.transcript: What's up guys! We're here in Melbourne, and in this video, we're going to show you our top things to do here. So hope you enjoy it! The vibrant city of Melbourne is located on the southeastern coast of Australia in the state of Victoria. Renowned as Australia's cultural capital, the city is home to a thriving arts scene. It also has a deeply ingrained coffee culture and a range of entertaining festivals and events. Melbourne is consistently ranked among the "world's most livable cities" and it's easy to see why. After spending 10 days here, we'll share our top things to do in and around the city. These include a range of free activities as well as can't miss paid experiences. At the end, we'll also share a few travel tips. Now let's dive in! We are Zac and Ine. We make helpful videos about destinations around the world so you can easily plan your own adventures. Brighton is a beautiful suburb located about 11 km or 6.8 mi to the southeast of the city center. The coastline here is home to the iconic beach boxes, which originally served as modest changing rooms. Today, these small structures have evolved into sought-after pieces of real estate and are mainly used for storing beach essentials. Lining the shores, the boxes are painted in a creative mix of colors and designs. This visually striking and photogenic aspect is why they have become one of Melbourne's most famous symbols. But beyond taking photos, you can also go for a leisurely stroll or take a refreshing dip in the cold waters. Just to the south of Melbourne's city center stands the majestic Shrine of Remembrance. This memorial was initially built to honor the State of Victoria's men and women who served in World War I. It has now evolved to commemorate all Australians who have served in a war, conflict, or peacekeeping operation. The Shrine is free to enter and once inside, you'll find galleries, medals, historical books, and much more. Ascending to the upper terrace is an absolute must as it offers a breathtaking panoramic view of Melbourne's cityscape. The city has a wealth of museums for all different types of interests. These offer enriching indoor activities and are great options for rainy days or if you just want to learn something new. We explored the Ian Potter Center which is adjacent to Federation Square. This is the Australian art section of the National Gallery of Victoria. It's free to visit its permanent collection. The National Gallery of Victoria is actually split into two locations, with the International Collection being a short walk away. But Melbourne offers many other museums as well and we've left a list of those on the screen here for you. Melbourne serves as a gateway to many incredible coastal and mountain landscapes, most of which can be visited on a day trip. We chose for convenient proximity with a day trip to the Dandenong Ranges and Phillip Island. The tour started with a ride aboard the Puffing Billy steam train from Belgrave to Lakeside, which lasted about an hour. Later, we traveled to Phillip Island where we stopped at various viewpoints such as The Nobbies. It was amazing to see so much wildlife thriving on the island, but the highlight was witnessing hundreds of little penguins returning to their burrows after sunset. We weren't allowed to film this moment but included some pictures from the Penguin Parade Center. You also have lots of other day trip options from the city such as Wilson's Promontory National Park, the Yarra Wine Valley, and the Great Ocean Road. Just keep in mind that many of these involve an extensive amount of driving or sitting on the bus if you want to make it back to the city in the same day. Located within a short walking distance of the Central Business District and adjacent to the Shrine of Remembrance, the Royal Botanic Gardens allow you to leave behind the city's hustle and bustle. Here, you can unwind with a delightful picnic on the lush lawns, enjoy a leisurely punt ride on Ornamental Lake, or wander through the diverse landscapes. You'll find everything from towering fern gullies to exotic palm tree groves inside these gardens. There's also a little café next to the lake if you're in need of a refreshment. Allow at least an hour to fully enjoy the tranquility and beauty of these remarkable gardens. Explore Melbourne's unique cultural pulse through its dynamic street art, which is largely found in the CBD. Melbourne has wholeheartedly embraced street art, channeling its bursting creativity into a constantly evolving outdoor gallery. Famous laneways to take in the art include Hosier, AC/DC, Higson, Duckboard, and Presgrave. Wander these accessible alleys to discover diverse artistic expressions, from large-scale colorful murals and intricate stencils to paste-ups and spontaneous graffiti. We actually explored the street art scene of the city as a guided experience. This was run by a local artist so it was really interesting to get an understanding of it from his perspective, which brings us to the sponsor of today's video. GetYourGuide is the leading online travel experience platform, with more than 110,000 curated things to do in 150 countries. These include everything from exclusive attractions to off-the-beaten path gems. In Melbourne alone there are over 400 experiences to choose from. Each of these are rated and reviewed by fellow travelers just like us, helping you find the perfect fit. Best of all, you can say goodbye to paper tickets - Everything you need is conveniently located within their App, making exploration seamless from start to finish. We particularly enjoyed our street art experience, where a local artist guide shared insights and the stories behind the artworks, even showing us the studio he works at. Plus, GetYourGuide made booking our Phillip Island day trip and Yarra River cruise a breeze. With their 24/7 customer support and flexible cancellation policy, you can book with confidence knowing you can adjust plans if needed. Based on our positive experiences, we highly recommend checking out GetYourGuide for your next adventure. Nestled in the heart of Melbourne's city center, the Queen Victoria Market is an enormous marketplace with a vibrant atmosphere. It's a must- visit destination for food lovers, boasting a fresh produce section with seasonal and often locally-sourced goods. Beyond the colorful displays of fruits and vegetables, you'll discover a souvenir section, meat and fish halls, a vast deli area, and a busy food court where you can grab a bite to eat. We indulged in some delicious Sri Lankan cuisine ourselves. Located on the 88th floor of the Eureka Tower in Southbank, the Melbourne Skydeck offers the best elevated views. It's the tallest observation deck in the southern hemisphere, soaring 285 m or 935 ft above the city. What makes it unique are the unparalleled panoramic views, stretching 360° from the southern beaches to the northern suburbs and the Yara River. You can enjoy both indoor viewing and an outdoor terrace, perfect for unobstructed photography. Arrive well before the sun goes down for a quieter experience. The evening crowds gather around sunset, making it hard to capture the twilight through the windows. Located just a short tram ride south of Melbourne CBD, St Kilda is a vibrant seaside suburb renowned for its bohemian charm and laid-back vibes. Here, you can relax on the beach or stroll along the famous St Kilda Pier, which offers some great skyline views. At dusk, you might even spot resident little penguins here. You can also find Melbourne's classic theme park in this neighborhood, which opened in 1912 and is a very family-friendly place to visit. We particularly enjoyed Acland Street as it buzzes with quirky shops, cafés, and patisseries. Situated directly across the Yara River from the city center, the Southbank Prominade is a pedestrian-friendly precinct. This dynamic area has many cozy restaurants, bars, and entertainment venues, making it a hub of activity. But you can also take in the beautiful river views or simply enjoy a relaxed stroll to soak in the atmosphere. If you have some extra time, we definitely recommend walking beyond the Southbank to the South Warf, because there's also a really good atmosphere here as well as some nice city views. The Southbank also comes alive in the evening with beautiful sunset views and the city skyline illuminating with glistening lights along the riverbanks. The iconic Melbourne Cricket Ground, which is also referred to as "The G", is located just a short distance east of the CBD. Attending an Aussie Rules Football match is an unmissable local experience. With 10 of the 18 AFL teams coming from Victoria, footy is practically a religion here. During our visit, we caught a Melbourne versus Richmond AFL game and the energy of the roaring crowd and the fast-paced action on the field was truly amazing. Footy games are generally held between March and September, so if you're visiting during summer then make sure to stop in for a cricket match instead. And if you happen to be in the city during an unusually quiet period, you can still visit the stadium on a guided tour. Flowing through the heart of the city, the Yarra River offers a unique perspective of Melbourne. And there's no better way to experience it than by captaining your own electric boat. Unlike typical guided tours, this self-drive option provides complete freedom to explore the river at your own pace. Departing from Banana Alley on the riverbank, you'll glide silently and slowly underneath various historic bridges. Pack a picnic or drinks and enjoy the iconic skyline from a truly unique vantage point. We timed our rental with golden hour, which made it even more magical. Now we'll share a few travel tips for Melbourne. There's a couple main zones to consider for your accommodation if you want to easily do the things we mentioned in this video. For a blend of convenience and a slightly more relaxed atmosphere, we recommend staying around the Southbank. This area offers easy access to major attractions and is located just across the Yarra River from the CBD. You'll find a variety of hotels and apartments here, often with lovely river and city views. The CBD is the most central area with both high-end and budget-friendly accommodation. It also has the easiest public transportation connections. Melbourne has an efficient public transportation network consisting of trams, trains, and buses. The easiest way to pay for your travel is with a myki card. You can purchase this card from train stations, some tram stops, and convenience stores. Remember to tap your card when boarding and disembarking. Melbourne also has a Free Tram Zone within the CBD and Docklands, which allows you to travel on any tram for free within the zone boundaries. Melbourne is generally a safe city for tourists. However, like any major urban area, it's wise to remain aware of your surroundings especially at night or in crowded places. Avoid the small and narrow alleyways at night. The emergency number in Australia is 0000 in case you need it. If you have more time in Melbourne or are looking for other things to do, we put a list of more options on the screen here for you. Welcome to Melbourne, Australia, one of the most vibrant and cultural cities in the world. In this video, we'll take you through some of the top things to do during your visit to Melbourne.

Melbourne is famous for its hidden laneways, which are home to a range of street art and graffiti. Take a stroll through Hosier Lane or AC/DC Lane to see some of the best street art in the city.

For a peaceful escape, visit the Royal Botanic Gardens and Fitzroy Gardens. These gardens offer a tranquil retreat from the hustle and bustle of the city, with stunning gardens, lakes, and walking paths.

Take a day trip to the Yarra Valley to experience some of the best wine, food, and scenery in the region. The Yarra Valley is just an hour's drive from Melbourne and offers visitors the chance to explore some of Australia's best wineries, restaurants, and nature reserves.

Explore the Alfred Nicholas Gardens for a fairytale-like experience. These gardens are home to stunning waterfalls, lakes, and walking trails that are sure to take your breath away.

Go tobogganing at Lake Mountain for an exhilarating winter adventure. Lake Mountain is just a two-hour drive from Melbourne and offers visitors the chance to experience the best winter sports in the region.

Take in the view from Eureka Tower Skydeck, the highest public viewing platform in the Southern Hemisphere. From the top, you can enjoy breathtaking 360-degree views of Melbourne and the surrounding areas.

Explore the National Gallery of Victoria, Australia's oldest and largest public art museum. The gallery features an extensive collection of Australian and international art, with works from some of the world's most renowned artists.

Go bushwalking in the Dandenong Ranges, a stunning nature reserve located just an hour's drive from Melbourne.

These are just a few of the many incredible things to do in Melbourne. Whether you're interested in art, food, culture, or nature, there's something for everyone in this amazing city. So what are you waiting for? Plan your trip to Melbourne today and experience all that this incredible city has to offer!

videos.title: ULTIMATE Travel Guide to Melbourne Australia 2026
videos.description:
Melbourne is one of the world's greatest cities and yes, one of its most liveable. It is also one of the world's best travel destinations and it offers the visitor an amazing number of attractions. This video details how to get around the city, where to eat, where to stay, where to get great coffee (many consider Melbourne the greatest coffee city on earth!), what there is to do and see in the city and the best day trips outside of Melbourne.

Stuff I LOVE:
eSim (necessary for international travel - 5% discount): https://holafly.sjv.io/jeeRO5
Backpacks and Bags (db Journey - the absolute best!): https://dbjourney.com/TRAVELEAZ

I hope you enjoy this video and please LIKE and SUBSCRIBE if you do.  More videos about Melbourne and Australia (and New Zealand) coming soon.  Thanks!

VRBO Link: https://www.vrbo.com/9075349ha?rm1=a2&expediaPropertyId=34017906

I admit to having a bias about Melbourne as I was born here and spent the first 11 years of my life in the City.  I visit often and know the city like the back of my hand (although I do admit to having a hard time keeping up with the many changes in Melbourne over the years).

Some of the many attractions discussed in this video include the Queen Victoria Market, Sporting Events (tennis, Formula 1, cricket and the magnificent Aussie Rules Football), the Melbourne Zoo and Sealife, art and architecture and the famous Melbourne parks. The attractions outside of the city include the Great Ocean Road, the Mornington Peninsula, Phillip Island (the Penguin Parade), Puffing Billy and the Healesville Sanctuary.

More about Melbourne:
Melbourne, Australia, stands as a vibrant and captivating destination that seamlessly blends urban sophistication with a relaxed cultural charm. Renowned for its diverse culinary scene, thriving arts and music culture, and stunning architecture, Melbourne offers a rich tapestry of experiences that make it an irresistible city to visit.

One of Melbourne's most distinctive features is its reputation as a food lover's paradise. The city boasts an eclectic array of dining options, ranging from trendy cafes serving up artisanal coffee and breakfast delights to upscale restaurants offering innovative global cuisine. Wander through bustling food markets like Queen Victoria Market, where fresh produce, local delicacies, and international flavors converge. The city's multicultural population is reflected in its dining offerings, ensuring that every palate is catered to.

Melbourne's artistic spirit is palpable, with a thriving arts scene that embraces both traditional and contemporary forms. The iconic National Gallery of Victoria showcases an extensive collection of art spanning centuries and continents, while the street art scene in neighborhoods like Fitzroy and Collingwood transforms the city's walls into open-air galleries. The Melbourne Arts Precinct, anchored by the Arts Centre Melbourne and the Australian Centre for Contemporary Art, serves as a hub for theatrical performances, concerts, exhibitions, and other cultural events.

Sporting enthusiasts will find Melbourne to be a haven as well. The city is synonymous with major international sporting events, such as the Australian Open, one of the Grand Slam tennis tournaments. The Melbourne Cricket Ground (MCG) holds a revered place in the hearts of sports fans and hosts cricket matches, Australian Rules Football games, and concerts. The passion for sports is deeply ingrained in the local culture, making attending a live event an exhilarating experience.

Melbourne's design and architecture contribute to its unique appeal. The city seamlessly integrates historic landmarks with contemporary structures. The Royal Exhibition Building and Carlton Gardens, a UNESCO World Heritage Site, showcases stunning 19th-century architecture. On the other hand, Federation Square's bold and modern design has become a meeting point and cultural hub. The city's blend of old and new provides an intriguing visual landscape for exploration.

Beyond its urban offerings, Melbourne serves as a gateway to natural beauty. Just a short drive away, the Great Ocean Road unveils breathtaking coastal vistas and iconic rock formations like the Twelve Apostles. Yarra Valley, renowned for its wineries and lush landscapes, offers a relaxing retreat for wine enthusiasts and nature lovers alike.
